title: Astronauts Return Safely from Moon Mission
summary: Four astronauts from NASA's Artemis II mission have returned safely to Earth after a trip around the Moon. They landed in the Pacific Ocean using a spacecraft called Orion, which traveled very fast and got very hot when coming back. The astronauts are named Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. They are now on a ship and will go home to see their families. This mission helps NASA plan to send people to live on the Moon in the future. Everyone was happy when they landed safely.

questions.correct_answer: Build a base on the Moon

questions.question: How did the astronauts feel after landing?

background_read: The Artemis program is NASA's plan to send people back to the Moon. This mission, Artemis II, was a test to fly around the Moon without landing. Astronauts are trained to live and work in space. Spacecraft like Orion are designed to protect astronauts during launch and return. Landing in the ocean is a safe way to come back to Earth. This success helps scientists learn more for future space adventures.
Article_Structure: WHO: Four astronauts: Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen
WHAT: They returned safely from a mission around the Moon in the Orion spacecraft
WHERE: They landed in the Pacific Ocean and are on a ship called USS John P Murtha
WHY: To test space travel and help NASA plan to build a base on the Moon

perspectives.description: The mission focused on keeping astronauts safe during a dangerous return to Earth.
